title: Ebareebaveebeedee
content:

{{Quote|Greetings, ill-favored stranger ones! The Illustrious Chieftain of Junkyards, Commander of the ''Thrifty'', King Ebareebaveebeedee bids you welcome to his Momship.|Ebareebaveebeedee's honor guard welcoming visitors|<ref name="SH" />}}
'''Ebareebaveebeedee''' was the [[Squib/Legends|Squib]] [[King/Legends|king]] of [[Skor II]] from the last [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] into the time of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. An adamant supporter of the Republic, Ebareebaveebeedee publicly proclaimed to [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in the midst of the wave of secessions that formed the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]] that the Squibs would not leave the Republic at any cost. When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] broke out, Ebareebaveebeedee found his world [[Battle of Skor II|under attack]] by the forces of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. Skor II was liberated by [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], and following the battle the king bestowed Windu with the honorary title "[[Big Time Hero of Beyond-Squib Eliteness]]" in a public ceremony.

Ebareebaveebeedee adapted to the rise of the Galactic Empire following the Clone Wars, making a deal with the Empire that stationed Squibs as laborers on Imperial [[Starship/Legends|ships]], who were then able to pass on the sites of good salvage operations to their fellow Squibs. In this time Ebareebaveebeedee kept his throne on the Squib [[Momship]] ''[[Thrifty]]''. When the Empire bombarded [[Dankayo/Legends|Dankayo]], the king struck a deal with [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] agents traveling in the [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] ''[[Bonderium]]'', who sought to recover vital [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|rebel intelligence]] from the Imperial transport ship [[Elusive (transport)|''Elusive'']]. In exchange for the coordinates of the ''Elusive'', Ebareebaveebeedee wanted them to steal a [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projector]] from the Squibs' archenemies, the [[Ugor/Legends|Ugors]], an act that would drive the Ugors into chaos that the Squibs could take advantage of. The crew of the ''Bonderium'' was successful, and in the wake of their victory Ebareebaveebeedee declared an [[Unidentified Squib pact|alliance]] of his government with the Rebellion.

==Biography==
===The Clone Wars===
{{Quote|Our ultrastrong loyalties and royalties to Republicness and whatever it may stand for will not let splitty-types coax-haggle Squibbish into leaving, no matter the size of the haggleprize.|Ebareebaveebeedee, on the Separatist Crisis|<ref name="HNN1" />}}
Ebareebaveebeedee became the [[King/Legends|king]] of the [[Squib Polyanarchy]] at some time before [[40 BBY/Legends|40 BBY]].<ref name="SH" /> The [[Monarchy/Legends|monarch]] was the sole authority over a number of distant, scattered communities that made up the Polyanarchy, and was a revered ruler,<ref name="DB">{{DB|species|squib|Squib}}</ref> raising the Squibs to levels of prosperity they had never experienced before.<ref name="SH" /> In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], as the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] was being wracked with a wave of secessions in the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], Ebareebaveebeedee made three [[Grand Proclamation]]s from the capital of [[Metrobig City]] over the course of three days. In his second, the [[Squib/Legends|Squib]] king declared the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] to be "[[koovy]]." He followed this up the next day with a twenty-minute address on Skor II's loyalty to the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] and Republic. Ebareebaveebeedee was emphatic in his statement that the Squibs were "going nowhere," insisting that no matter how much was offered by the secessionists, his people were too loyal to the Republic to be coaxed into leaving it. At the close of his twenty minutes, Ebareebaveebeedee bargained for another six minutes of airtime, which he used to add "You bet."<ref name="HNN1" />

[[File:Mace_Windu_statue_on_Skor_II.jpg|thumb|left|175px|Ebareebaveebeedee honors Mace Windu.]]
Despite the attempts of Ebareebaveebeedee and others to hold the Republic together, the secessionist crisis eventually resulted in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. A year and a half after the King's Grand Proclamation, Skor II was invaded by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], who intended to use the world as a base to plant [[Proximity mine/Legends|proximity mines]] along the [[Rimma Trade Route/Legends|Rimma Trade Route]]. [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] led the Republic counterattack, freeing the [[Metrobig Interplanetary Blastport]] from the Confederacy's [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]]. After the battle, Ebareebaveebeedee, in honor of Windu's exploits in the battle, declared him an honorary [[Big Time Hero of Beyond-Squib Eliteness]] and revealed a [[Bronzium/Legends|bronzium]] [[Statue of Mace Windu|statue]] of Windu to a crowd of joyous Squibs. Ebareebaveebeedee then made a speech declaring that Windu's actions would be remembered throughout Squib history. Windu had left Skor II by this time, leaving behind a [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] contingent to guard against future Confederate activity, and the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] made no comment on Ebareebaveebeedee's ceremony.<ref name="HNN2">{{InsiderCite|69|Republic HoloNet News Core Edition 14:9:01}}</ref>

===The Galactic Civil War===
{{Quote|So, dealers with abridged decks, we of Lofty Squib greet you in name of universal buddy-buddiness, irrespectful of differences in station, no less.|Ebareebaveebeedee greets a group of Rebels|<ref name="SH" />}}
Ebareebaveebeedee remained king as the Republic gave way to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. The king eventually made a deal with the new government in which Squibs would serve aboard Imperial [[Starship/Legends|starships]] doing menial labor—an arrangement the Empire considered to be [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]]—and in return, unknown to the Empire, the Squibs would surreptitiously send messages to the [[Squib Reclamation Fleet]] giving the locations of Imperial dumping grounds. The Squibs would then salvage these dumping grounds for any useful material.<ref name="DB" /> Styled "Illustrious Chieftain of Junkyards," Ebareebaveebeedee was in this time [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the ''[[Thrifty]]'', the Squib [[Momship]] and the seat of his throne. The king's throne itself was made from the cockpit of a [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunter]], and it was located in an audience chamber decorated with baubles and a mural composed of [[Tax stamp/Legends|Imperial tax stamps]]. He wore a collection of garbage passed down between generations of kings, all items of which he was forbidden by [[High Squibbian Law]] to use.<ref name="SH" />

[[File:UgorShip.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The ''Thrifty'' engages an Ugorian ''Black Hole''-class Salvage Dreadnought.]]
At some point between [[0 ABY/Legends|0]] and [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]],<ref name="date">[[Scavenger Hunt (adventure)|''Scavenger Hunt'']] takes place at an unspecified point after the destruction of the first [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], which occurred in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', and before the assignment of [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]] to [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]], which occurred in [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]] according to ''The New Essential Chronology''</ref> the Empire [[Battle of Dankayo|attacked]] a [[Dankayo Base|Rebel base]] on the planet [[Dankayo/Legends|Dankayo]]. The Imperial transport [[Elusive (transport)|''Elusive'']] was left behind to perform mop-up operations, only to be interrupted by the arrival of the [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] ''[[Bonderium]]'', under the control of a group of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]]. After a brief battle, the ''Elusive'' dumped its garbage and fled into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]; the ''Thrifty'', which had been alerted to the location of the trash dump by their Squib agent aboard the ''Elusive'', [[Dono (Squib)|Dono]], arrived shortly thereafter and began gathering trash from the departed Imperial vessel. Within moments, a [[Black Hole-class Salvage Dreadnought|''Black Hole''-class Salvage Dreadnought]] belonging to the [[Ugor/Legends|Ugor]]—a species with a long rivalry with the Squibs—arrived as well and began attacking both vessels. It was not interested in destroying them, though, only holding them off long enough to collect trash for itself, and it soon fled.<ref name="SH" />

The ''Thrifty'' had been monitoring the earlier battle between the Rebels and Imperials, had detected that the ''Bonderium'' was not Imperial, and had learned from the Rebel vessel's scans of the ''Elusive''{{'s}} trajectory that its passengers had an interest in locating the Imperial ship. When a new message from Dono came in, detailing the next stop of the ''Elusive'' in code (actually the language [[Old High Squibbian]]), the ''Thrifty'' relayed the message on an open channel, thereby letting the Rebels know that the Squibs could lead them to the ''Elusive''. The ''Bonderium'' made contact with the ''Thrifty'', and after some posturing on the part of the Squibs, the Rebels were invited aboard by the king to make a deal. Ebareebaveebeedee's messengers greeted them at the [[Airlock/Legends|airlock]], and the Rebel party was escorted to the king's audience chamber.<ref name="SH" />

[[File:Ebareebaveebeedee_profile_SH.jpg|thumb|left|175px|Ebareebaveebeedee during the Galactic Civil War]]
The Rebels, as it turned out, sought a collection of [[Datacard/Legends|datapacks]] that the ''Elusive'' had taken from Dankayo, which held information on Rebel operations throughout three sectors. They needed to intercept the ''Elusive'' before it brought the data to [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]], and after extensive haggling Ebareebaveebeedee made a deal with them. The Rebels would retrieve an unidentified "quest object" from the [[Paradise system/Legends|Paradise system]], home of the Ugors. Lacking in any planets, the Paradise system consisted of a motley collection of trash held together by the gravitational forces of a [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projector]] that was once part of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]]. Unknown to the Rebels, it was this that Ebareebaveebeedee sought; stealing the projector would throw the Ugors into chaos, a desirable goal to the king. He sent with them a Squib guide, [[Spilferithimus-narlamos]], to identify the object and the cooking droid [[L9-G8]], presented to them as a gift for closing the bargain but actually a practical joke by the king. When the Rebels deactivated and took the projector, the system began to collapse, and the ''Thrifty'', along with a fleet of Squib ships, arrived to scavenge whatever they could from the suddenly disorganized Ugors. Ebareebaveebeedee hailed the victorious Rebels and invited them aboard again to conclude their deal.<ref name="SH" />

In their absence, the king had had more murals added to his walls, this time of the collapse of the Paradise system, and had invited a [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] trader to join in his negotiations with the Rebels. Another round of haggling began, eventually ending in the Rebels gaining possession of the coordinates of the ''Elusive'' and the projector, which they could use to ambush the ship and pull it from hyperspace. Ebareebaveebeedee sent them on their way with a holographic message to be played only once they had defeated the ''Elusive'' and reported to the Alliance. In it, the king announced an [[Unidentified Squib pact|alliance]] between the Rebellion and Squib Polyanarchy, with the Rebels of the ''Bonderium'' to serve as [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassadors]] to the Squibs.<ref name="SH" />

==Personality and traits==
Ebareebaveebeedee was a traditionalist who held firmly to the ancient High Squibbian customs, relying on them over his personal whims. Fair and just, he honored his word<ref name="SH" /> and was revered by his people.<ref name="DB" /> During the Separatist Crisis, he demonstrated this devotion when he made a vow of loyalty to the Republic, declaring that his promise would not be broken for any price.<ref name="HNN1" /> He was also fond of lengthy, often whimsical proclamations, which reflected both his eccentricity and his leadership within Squib culture.<ref name="CE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> Though outright lying was forbidden in Squib bargains, he could still conceal the truth when it suited his ends. One such instance was when he directed the Rebels to recover a gravity well projector, not because he wanted the artifact itself, but because of the disruptive effect it would have on the rival Ugors who used it.<ref name="SH" />

At the time of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]’s rule, the aged yet shrewd ruler Ebareebaveebeedee masterminded the placement of Squib workers as spies aboard capital ships, granting his diminutive scavenger species an advantage in salvaging the space junk ejected from them.<ref name="CE" /> Like other Squibs, the king greatly enjoyed haggling, preferring a deal to be as complicated as possible.<ref name="SH" /> He was even known to bargain during his political addresses for more airtime.<ref name="HNN1" /> Ebareebaveebeedee also had a sense of humor, found in his giving of impractical gifts during bargains as a joke.<ref name="SH" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ebareebaveebeedee was created for the [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]] adventure [[Scavenger Hunt (adventure)|''Scavenger Hunt'']], by [[Brad Freeman]], in [[1989]]. He was not mentioned for another 13 years, until [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and [[Paul Ens]] included him in a [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News]] entry as part of the buildup to [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']]. They later used the character again in an entry of [[HoloNet News (Insider)|HoloNet News]], this time set during the Clone Wars and appearing in ''[[Star Wars Insider]]''.

"Eebareebaveebeedee" [''sic''] is included as an example name for Squibs in the ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'', which mentions that it can be shortened to "Eebaree."
